727	func decodeToNamedSlice(value string, target interface{}) error {
728		targetVal := reflect.ValueOf(target)
729		if targetVal.Kind() != reflect.Ptr || targetVal.Elem().Kind() != reflect.Slice {
730			return fmt.Errorf("target must be a pointer to a slice")
731		}
732		targetSlice := targetVal.Elem()
733		elemType := targetSlice.Type().Elem()
734	
735		if value == "" {
736			// If the value is empty, leave the slice as it is.
737			return nil
738		}
739		if strings.HasPrefix(value, "[") && strings.HasSuffix(value, "]") {
740			// Unmarshal JSON array into a temporary slice
741			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(value), target); err != nil {
742				return fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal JSON array: %w", err)
743			}
744		} else {
745			// Parse space-separated values
746			for _, item := range strings.Split(value, " ") {
747				elem := reflect.New(elemType).Elem()
748				if elem.Kind() == reflect.Struct {
749					field := elem.FieldByName("Name")
750					if field.IsValid() && field.Kind() == reflect.String {
751						field.SetString(item)
752					}
753				}
754				targetSlice.Set(reflect.Append(targetSlice, elem))
755			}
756		}
757		return nil
758	}

82	func ManagedStreamingIAM(authFn func(context.Context) (Auth, error)) sasl.Mechanism {
83		return mskiam(authFn)
84	}
85	
86	func (mskiam) Name() string { return "AWS_MSK_IAM" }
87	
88	func (fn mskiam) Authenticate(ctx context.Context, host string) (sasl.Session, []byte, error) {
89		auth, err := fn(ctx)
90		if err != nil {
91			return nil, nil, err
92		}
93	
94		challenge, err := challenge(auth, host)
95		if err != nil {
96			return nil, nil, err
97		}
98	
99		return new(session), challenge, nil
100	}
101	
102	type session struct{}
103	
104	func (session) Challenge(resp []byte) (bool, []byte, error) {
105		if len(resp) == 0 {
106			return false, nil, errors.New("empty challenge response: failed")
107		}
108		return true, nil, nil
109	}

111	const service = "kafka-cluster"
112	
113	func challenge(auth Auth, host string) ([]byte, error) {
114		host, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(host) // we do not need the port
115		if err != nil {
116			return nil, err
117		}
118		region, err := identifyRegion(host)
119		if err != nil {
120			return nil, err
121		}
122	
123		var (
124			timestamp = time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
125			date      = timestamp[:8] // 20060102
126			scope     = scope(date, region)
127			v         = make(url.Values)
128		)
129	
130		v.Set("Action", service+":Connect")
131		v.Set("X-Amz-Algorithm", "AWS4-HMAC-SHA256")
132		v.Set("X-Amz-Credential", auth.AccessKey+"/"+scope)
133		v.Set("X-Amz-Date", timestamp)
134		v.Set("X-Amz-Expires", "300") // 5 min
135		v.Set("X-Amz-SignedHeaders", "host")
136		if auth.SessionToken != "" {
137			v.Set("X-Amz-Security-Token", auth.SessionToken)
138		}
139	
140		qps := strings.ReplaceAll(v.Encode(), "+", "%20")
141	
142		canonicalRequest := task1(host, qps)
143		sts := task2(timestamp, scope, canonicalRequest)
144		signature := task3(auth.SecretKey, region, date, sts)
145	
146		v.Set("X-Amz-Signature", signature) // task4
147	
148		// According to the Java source and manual testing, all values in our
149		// challenge map must be lowercased, and we MUST have host, and we MUST
150		// have version, and version MUST be 2020_10_22.
151		keyvals := make(map[string]string)
152		for key, values := range v {
153			keyvals[strings.ToLower(key)] = values[0]
154		}
155		keyvals["host"] = host
156		keyvals["version"] = "2020_10_22"
157		ua := auth.UserAgent
158		if ua == "" {
159			ua = strings.Join([]string{"franz-go", runtime.Version(), hostname}, "/")
160		}
161		keyvals["user-agent"] = ua
162	
163		marshaled, err := json.Marshal(keyvals)
164		if err != nil {
165			return nil, err
166		}
167		return marshaled, nil
168	}

170	// https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4-create-string-to-sign.html
171	// "CredentialScope", Part 3
172	func scope(date, region string) string {
173		return strings.Join([]string{date, region, service, "aws4_request"}, "/")
174	}
175	
176	// https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4-create-canonical-request.html
177	func task1(host, qps string) []byte {
178		// We start with our defined method, "GET", and the defined empty path,
179		// "/". For query parameters, we have to escape +'s with %20, but we did
180		// that above when building our URL.
181		//
182		//   HTTPRequestMethod + '\n' +
183		//   CanonicalURI + '\n' +
184		//   CanonicalQueryString + '\n' +
185		canon := make([]byte, 0, 200)
186		canon = append(canon, "GET\n"...)
187		canon = append(canon, "/\n"...)
188		canon = append(canon, qps...)
189		canon = append(canon, '\n')
190	
191		// We only sign one header, the host. Each signed header is followed by
192		// a newline, and then the canonical header block is followed itself by
193		// a newline.
194		//
195		//   CanonicalHeaders + '\n' +
196		//   SignedHeaders + '\n' +
197		canon = append(canon, "host:"...)
198		canon = append(canon, host...)
199		canon = append(canon, "\n\nhost\n"...)
200	
201		// Finally, we add our empty body.
202		//
203		//   HexEncode(Hash(RequestPayload))
204		const emptyBody = "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"
205		return append(canon, emptyBody...)
206	}
207	
208	// https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4-create-string-to-sign.html
209	func task2(timestamp, scope string, canonicalRequest []byte) []byte {
210		toSign := make([]byte, 0, 512)
211		toSign = append(toSign, "AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\n"...)
212		toSign = append(toSign, timestamp...)
213		toSign = append(toSign, '\n')
214		toSign = append(toSign, scope...)
215		toSign = append(toSign, '\n')
216		canonHash := sha256.Sum256(canonicalRequest)
217		hexBuf := make([]byte, 64) // 32 bytes to 64
218		hex.Encode(hexBuf, canonHash[:])
219		toSign = append(toSign, hexBuf...)
220		return toSign
221	}
222	
223	var aws4requestBytes = []byte("aws4_request")
224	
225	// https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/sigv4-calculate-signature.html
226	func task3(secretKey, region, date string, sts []byte) string {
227		key := make([]byte, 0, 100)
228		key = append(key, "AWS4"...)
229		key = append(key, secretKey...)
230	
231		h := hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
232		h.Write([]byte(date)) // kDate
233	
234		key = h.Sum(key[:0])
235		h = hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
236		h.Write([]byte(region)) // kRegion
237	
238		key = h.Sum(key[:0])
239		h = hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
240		h.Write([]byte(service)) // kService
241	
242		key = h.Sum(key[:0])
243		h = hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
244		h.Write(aws4requestBytes) // kSigning
245	
246		key = h.Sum(key[:0])
247		h = hmac.New(sha256.New, key)
248		h.Write(sts)
249	
250		return hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(key[:0]))
251	}
252	
253	// aws-java-sdk-core/src/main/resources/com/amazonaws/partitions/endpoints.json
254	var suffixes = []string{
255		".amazonaws.com",
256		".amazonaws.com.cn",
257		".c2s.ic.gov",
258		".sc2s.sgov.gov",
259	}
260	
261	// aws-java-sdk-core/src/main/java/com/amazonaws/partitions/PartitionMetadataProvider.java
262	// tryGetRegionByEndpointDnsSuffix
263	func identifyRegion(host string) (string, error) {
264		for _, suffix := range suffixes {
265			if strings.HasSuffix(host, suffix) {
266				serviceRegion := strings.TrimSuffix(host, suffix)
267				regionDot := strings.LastIndexByte(serviceRegion, '.')
268				if regionDot == -1 {
269					break
270				}
271				return serviceRegion[regionDot+1:], nil
272			}
273		}
274		return "", fmt.Errorf("cannot determine the region in %+q", host)
275	}

Now I have a thorough understanding. The `aws.Auth` struct supports `SessionToken`, which is used for STS temporary credentials. The SQS consumer already supports `AssumeRoleARN` and `CredentialsProfile`. I need to add the same to the Kafka consumer.

The approach:
1. Add `AssumeRoleARN` and `CredentialsProfile` fields to `KafkaConsumerConfig`
2. In `initClient()`, when `aws-msk-iam` SASL mechanism is used and `AssumeRoleARN` is set, use AWS STS to assume the role and get temporary credentials via `stscreds`, then use the `ManagedStreamingIAM` (the callback-based mechanism) to dynamically provide fresh credentials.

Let me also check the existing Kafka test file and the schema.json.

751		defer cancel()

789	func runConsumptionIteration(b *testing.B, topic string, numMessages int, iteration int) time.Duration {
790		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 300*time.Second)
791		defer cancel()
792	
793		messageCh := make(chan struct{}, numMessages)
794		consumerClosed := make(chan struct{})
795	
796		mockDispatcher := &MockDispatcher{
797			onDispatchCommand: func(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error {
798				messageCh <- struct{}{}
799				return nil
800			},
801		}
802	
803		config := KafkaConfig{
804			Brokers:               []string{testKafkaBrokerURL},
805			Topics:                []string{topic},
806			ConsumerGroup:         uuid.New().String(),
807			MaxPollRecords:        100,
808			FetchMaxBytes:         10 * 1024 * 1024, // 10 MB.
809			FetchMaxWait:          configtypes.Duration(200 * time.Millisecond),
810			PartitionQueueMaxSize: 1000,
811		}
812	
813		consumer, err := NewKafkaConsumer(
814			config, mockDispatcher, testCommon(prometheus.NewRegistry()))
815		if err != nil {
816			b.Fatal(err)
817		}
818	
819		b.Logf("iteration %d: starting consumer with group %s", iteration, config.ConsumerGroup)
820		startTime := time.Now()
821	
822		go func() {
823			err := consumer.Run(ctx)
824			if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
825				b.Logf("consumer error: %v", err)
826			}
827			close(consumerClosed)
828		}()
829	
830		// Wait for all messages to be consumed
831		processedCount := 0
832	
833		for j := 0; j < numMessages; j++ {
834			select {
835			case <-messageCh:
836				processedCount++
837				// Log progress every 100 messages.
838				if processedCount%100 == 0 {
839					elapsed := time.Since(startTime)
840					rate := float64(processedCount) / elapsed.Seconds()
841					b.Logf("iteration %d: processed %d/%d messages (%.0f msg/sec)", iteration, processedCount, numMessages, rate)
842				}
843			case <-time.After(60 * time.Second):
844				b.Fatal("timeout waiting for messages")
845			}
846		}
847	
848		consumptionTime := time.Since(startTime)
849		rate := float64(numMessages) / consumptionTime.Seconds()
850		b.Logf("iteration %d: consumed %d messages in %v (%.0f msg/sec)", iteration, numMessages, consumptionTime, rate)
851	
852		cancel()
853		select {
854		case <-consumerClosed:
855		case <-time.After(30 * time.Second):
856			b.Fatal("timeout waiting for consumer to close")
857		}
858	
859		return consumptionTime
860	}

870	func TestUnboundedQueue_BasicPushPopNumRecords(t *testing.T) {
871		q := newUnboundedQueue()
872		require.False(t, q.IsClosed(), "new queue should not be closed")
873		require.Equal(t, 0, q.NumRecords(), "new queue should have length 0")
874	
875		// Push items.
876		for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
877			ok := q.Push(makeFTP("topic", int32(i), 1))
878			require.True(t, ok, "push should succeed")
879			require.Equal(t, i+1, q.NumRecords(), "num records should increase after push")
880		}
881	
882		// Pop items in FIFO order.
883		for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {

886			require.Equal(t, "topic", item.Topic)
887			require.Equal(t, int32(i), item.Partition)
888			require.Equal(t, 5-(i+1), q.NumRecords(), "num records should decrease after pop")
889		}
890	
891		// Now empty.
892		item, ok := q.Pop()
893		require.False(t, ok, "pop on empty queue should return ok=false")
894		require.Equal(t, kgo.FetchTopicPartition{}, item, "item should be zero value when pop fails")
895		require.Equal(t, 0, q.NumRecords())
896	}

916		q := newUnboundedQueue()
917	
918		// 1) Initially no signal.
919		select {
920		case <-q.NotEmpty():
921			require.Fail(t, "NotEmpty channel should not signal before any push")
922		default:
923		}

929		require.True(t, q.Push(makeFTP("t", 1, 1)))
930		require.True(t, q.Push(makeFTP("t", 2, 1)))
931

935		case <-q.NotEmpty():

937		case <-time.After(50 * time.Millisecond):
938			require.Fail(t, "expected one signal after three pushes")

940	
941		// 5) After draining, channel must now be empty again.
942		select {
943		case <-q.NotEmpty():
944			require.Fail(t, "no extra signals until another push after drain")
945		default:
946		}
947	
948		// 6) One more push now signals again.
949		require.True(t, q.Push(makeFTP("t", 3, 1)))
950		select {
951		case <-q.NotEmpty():
952			// expected
953		case <-time.After(50 * time.Millisecond):
954			require.Fail(t, "push after drain should signal NotEmpty again")

959		q := newUnboundedQueue()
960		require.False(t, q.IsClosed())
961	
962		// Close first time.
963		q.Close()
964		require.True(t, q.IsClosed(), "queue should report closed after Close()")
965

967		require.NotPanics(t, q.Close, "calling Close() twice should not panic")
968		require.True(t, q.IsClosed(), "still closed after second Close()")
969

976		require.False(t, ok, "push after close should return false")
977	
978		// Pop always fails when closed, even if items were enqueued before
979		// Let's test with a fresh queue.
980		q2 := newUnboundedQueue()
981		q2.Push(makeFTP("y", 2, 1))
982		q2.Close()

984		item, popped := q2.Pop()
985		require.False(t, popped, "pop after close should return false")
986		require.Equal(t, kgo.FetchTopicPartition{}, item, "item should be zero on pop after close")

990		q := newUnboundedQueue()
991		const n = 100
992	
993		// Start n producers.
994		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
995			go func(i int) {
996				ok := q.Push(makeFTP("c", int32(i), 1))
997				require.True(t, ok)

1003		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
1004			go func() {
1005				for {
1006					item, ok := q.Pop()
1007					if !ok {
1008						if q.IsClosed() {
1009							// If queue is closed and no items left, exit.
1010							return
1011						}
1012						continue
1013					}
1014					results <- item
1015				}
1016			}()
1017		}
1018	
1019		// Collect.
1020		seen := make(map[int32]bool)
1021		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
1022			ftp := <-results
1023			require.False(t, seen[ftp.Partition], "each partition should be unique: %v", ftp.Partition)
1024			seen[ftp.Partition] = true
1025		}

1039		)
1040	
1041		type consumerHandle struct {
1042			cancel context.CancelFunc
1043			doneCh chan struct{}
1044		}
1045	
1046		type mode struct {
1047			name                 string
1048			instanceID           func(consumerIndex int) string
1049			leaveGroupOnShutdown bool // simulate old behavior: manual LeaveGroup for static members
1050		}
1051	
1052		modes := []mode{
1053			{
1054				name:                 "dynamic_instance_id",
1055				instanceID:           func(i int) string { return "centrifugo-" + uuid.New().String() },
1056				leaveGroupOnShutdown: true, // old behavior: manual LeaveGroup for dynamic static members
1057			},
1058			{
1059				name:       "no_instance_id",
1060				instanceID: func(i int) string { return "" },
1061			},
1062			{
1063				name:       "stable_instance_id",
1064				instanceID: func(i int) string { return fmt.Sprintf("centrifugo-stable-%d", i) },
1065			},
1066		}
1067	
1068		type restartResult struct {
1069			consumerIndex int
1070			delay         time.Duration
1071		}
1072	
1073		results := make(map[string][]restartResult)
1074	
1075		for _, m := range modes {
1076			m := m
1077			t.Run(m.name, func(t *testing.T) {
1078				ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 180*time.Second)
1079				defer cancel()
1080	
1081				topic := "rollout_test_" + uuid.New().String()
1082				group := "rollout_group_" + uuid.New().String()
1083	
1084				err := createTestTopic(ctx, topic, numPartitions, 1)
1085				require.NoError(t, err)
1086	
1087				// Start continuous producer that sends to all partitions frequently.
1088				producerCtx, producerCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
1089				defer producerCancel()
1090	
1091				producer, err := kgo.NewClient(
1092					kgo.SeedBrokers(testKafkaBrokerURL),
1093					kgo.RecordPartitioner(kgo.ManualPartitioner()),
1094				)
1095				require.NoError(t, err)
1096	
1097				go func() {
1098					defer producer.Close()
1099					for {
1100						select {
1101						case <-producerCtx.Done():
1102							return
1103						default:
1104						}
1105						for p := int32(0); p < numPartitions; p++ {
1106							producer.ProduceSync(producerCtx, &kgo.Record{
1107								Topic:     topic,
1108								Partition: p,
1109								Value:     []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`{"ts":%d}`, time.Now().UnixMilli())),
1110							})
1111						}
1112						time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
1113					}
1114				}()
1115	
1116				startConsumer := func(index int) (*consumerHandle, <-chan struct{}) {
1117					instanceID := m.instanceID(index)
1118					config := KafkaConfig{
1119						Brokers:       []string{testKafkaBrokerURL},
1120						Topics:        []string{topic},
1121						ConsumerGroup: group,
1122						InstanceID:    instanceID,
1123					}
1124	
1125					firstMessageCh := make(chan struct{})
1126					firstMessageReceived := atomic.Bool{}
1127	
1128					dispatcher := &MockDispatcher{
1129						onDispatchCommand: func(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error {
1130							if firstMessageReceived.CompareAndSwap(false, true) {
1131								close(firstMessageCh)
1132							}
1133							return nil
1134						},
1135					}
1136	
1137					consumer, consumerErr := NewKafkaConsumer(config, dispatcher, testCommon(prometheus.NewRegistry()))
1138					require.NoError(t, consumerErr)
1139					consumer.testOnlyConfig.leaveGroupOnShutdown = m.leaveGroupOnShutdown
1140	
1141					consumerCtx, consumerCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
1142					doneCh := make(chan struct{})
1143	
1144					go func() {
1145						_ = consumer.Run(consumerCtx)
1146						close(doneCh)
1147					}()
1148	
1149					return &consumerHandle{
1150						cancel: consumerCancel,
1151						doneCh: doneCh,
1152					}, firstMessageCh
1153				}
1154	
1155				// Start initial consumers and wait for each to be active.
1156				handles := make([]*consumerHandle, numConsumers)
1157				for i := 0; i < numConsumers; i++ {
1158					h, firstMsg := startConsumer(i)
1159					handles[i] = h
1160					select {
1161					case <-firstMsg:
1162						t.Logf("initial consumer %d is active", i)
1163					case <-time.After(60 * time.Second):
1164						t.Fatalf("timeout waiting for initial consumer %d to start processing", i)
1165					}
1166				}
1167	
1168				// Wait for steady state.
1169				time.Sleep(5 * time.Second)
1170				t.Log("steady state reached, starting rolling restart")
1171	
1172				// Rolling restart: stop and replace consumers one by one.
1173				var modeResults []restartResult
1174				for i := 0; i < numConsumers; i++ {
1175					restartStart := time.Now()
1176	
1177					// Stop old consumer.
1178					handles[i].cancel()
1179					select {
1180					case <-handles[i].doneCh:
1181					case <-time.After(30 * time.Second):
1182						t.Fatalf("timeout waiting for consumer %d to stop", i)
1183					}
1184					stopDuration := time.Since(restartStart)
1185	
1186					// Start replacement.
1187					var firstMsg <-chan struct{}
1188					handles[i], firstMsg = startConsumer(i)
1189	
1190					// Wait for replacement to process its first message.
1191					select {
1192					case <-firstMsg:
1193					case <-time.After(60 * time.Second):
1194						t.Fatalf("timeout waiting for replacement consumer %d to start processing", i)
1195					}
1196	
1197					delay := time.Since(restartStart)
1198					modeResults = append(modeResults, restartResult{consumerIndex: i, delay: delay})
1199					t.Logf("consumer %d: stop=%v, total restart delay=%v", i, stopDuration, delay)
1200	
1201					// Let things stabilize before next restart.
1202					time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
1203				}
1204				results[m.name] = modeResults
1205	
1206				// Cleanup.
1207				for i := 0; i < numConsumers; i++ {
1208					handles[i].cancel()
1209					<-handles[i].doneCh
1210				}
1211				producerCancel()
1212			})
1213		}
1214	
1215		// Print comparison summary.
1216		t.Log("\n========== ROLLING RESTART COMPARISON ==========")
1217		for _, m := range modes {
1218			modeResults := results[m.name]
1219			var total time.Duration
1220			for _, r := range modeResults {
1221				total += r.delay
1222			}
1223			t.Logf("\n--- %s ---", m.name)
1224			for _, r := range modeResults {
1225				t.Logf("  consumer %d restart: %v", r.consumerIndex, r.delay)
1226			}
1227			t.Logf("  TOTAL rollout delay: %v", total)
1228		}
1229		t.Log("================================================")
1230	}
1231	
1232	// TestKafkaConsumer_CommitsOffsetsOnShutdown verifies that offsets marked just before
1233	// shutdown are actually committed to Kafka. After the consumer processes all messages
1234	// and is shut down, we use kadm to check that committed offsets match the number of
1235	// produced messages.
1236	func TestKafkaConsumer_CommitsOffsetsOnShutdown(t *testing.T) {
1237		t.Parallel()
1238		testKafkaTopic := "centrifugo_consumer_test_" + uuid.New().String()
1239		consumerGroup := uuid.New().String()
1240		const numMessages = 5
1241	
1242		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
1243		defer cancel()
1244	
1245		err := createTestTopic(ctx, testKafkaTopic, 1, 1)
1246		require.NoError(t, err)
1247	
1248		// Produce messages before starting the consumer.
1249		for i := 0; i < numMessages; i++ {
1250			err = produceTestMessage(testKafkaTopic, []byte(fmt.Sprintf(`{"msg":%d}`, i)), nil)
1251			require.NoError(t, err)
1252		}
1253	
1254		allProcessed := make(chan struct{})
1255		var processedCount atomic.Int32
1256		consumerClosed := make(chan struct{})
1257	
1258		consumerCtx, consumerCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
1259	
1260		config := KafkaConfig{
1261			Brokers:       []string{testKafkaBrokerURL},
1262			Topics:        []string{testKafkaTopic},
1263			ConsumerGroup: consumerGroup,
1264		}
1265	
1266		consumer, err := NewKafkaConsumer(config, &MockDispatcher{
1267			onDispatchCommand: func(ctx context.Context, method string, data []byte) error {
1268				if int(processedCount.Add(1)) == numMessages {
1269					close(allProcessed)
1270				}
1271				return nil
1272			},
1273		}, testCommon(prometheus.NewRegistry()))
1274		require.NoError(t, err)
1275	
1276		go func() {
1277			_ = consumer.Run(consumerCtx)
1278			close(consumerClosed)
1279		}()
1280	
1281		waitCh(t, allProcessed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for all messages to be processed")
1282	
1283		// Shut down consumer — this should commit marked offsets.
1284		consumerCancel()
1285		waitCh(t, consumerClosed, 30*time.Second, "timeout waiting for consumer to close")
1286	
1287		// Check committed offsets via kadm.
1288		cl, err := kgo.NewClient(kgo.SeedBrokers(testKafkaBrokerURL))
1289		require.NoError(t, err)
1290		defer cl.Close()
1291	
1292		admClient := kadm.NewClient(cl)
1293		defer admClient.Close()
1294	
1295		offsets, err := admClient.FetchOffsets(ctx, consumerGroup)
1296		require.NoError(t, err)
1297	
1298		offset, ok := offsets.Lookup(testKafkaTopic, 0)
1299		require.True(t, ok, "committed offset not found for topic %s partition 0", testKafkaTopic)
1300		require.Equal(t, int64(numMessages), offset.At,
1301			"committed offset should equal number of produced messages")
1302	}
1303	
1304	func BenchmarkKafkaConsumer_ConsumePreLoadedTopic(b *testing.B) {
1305		const (
1306			numPartitions = 10
1307			numMessages   = 100000
1308		)
1309	
1310		testKafkaTopic := "consumer_bench_" + uuid.New().String()
1311		prePopulateTopic(b, testKafkaTopic, numPartitions, numMessages)
1312	
1313		b.ResetTimer()
1314	
1315		var totalTime time.Duration
1316		for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
1317			iterationTime := runConsumptionIteration(b, testKafkaTopic, numMessages, i+1)
1318			totalTime += iterationTime
1319		}
1320	
1321		if b.N > 0 {
1322			avgTime := totalTime / time.Duration(b.N)
1323			avgRate := float64(numMessages) / avgTime.Seconds()
1324			b.Logf("benchmark complete: %d iterations, avg time %v, avg rate %.0f msg/sec", b.N, avgTime, avgRate)
1325		}
1326	}

Now update `kafka.go` to use STS AssumeRole. The approach:
1. Add imports for AWS SDK config and stscreds (matching SQS pattern)
2. In `initClient()`, when `sasl_mechanism` is `aws-msk-iam` and `AssumeRoleARN` is set, load AWS config with the profile/region, then use STS to assume the role, and use the `ManagedStreamingIAM` callback mechanism (instead of `AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism`) to dynamically provide credentials.

The key difference from the static approach: instead of using `aws.Auth{...}.AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism()` (which returns fixed credentials), we'll use `aws.ManagedStreamingIAM(func(context.Context)(aws.Auth, error){...})` which calls back for fresh credentials each time.

But wait - the `ManagedStreamingIAM` callback approach is for cases where you need credential rotation. For STS assume role, we should fetch credentials once (or use a cached provider). Let me look at how the SQS consumer does it:

In SQS, it creates an `sts.Client`, an `stscreds.NewAssumeRoleProvider`, wraps it in `aws.NewCredentialsCache`, and sets `awsCfg.Credentials`. Then SQS SDK uses those credentials automatically.

For Kafka with franz-go, we need to extract the actual credentials values (access key, secret key, session token) from the AWS credentials provider and put them into `aws.Auth`. Let me code this up.

VERSION := $(shell git describe --tags | sed -e 's/^v//g' | awk -F "-" '{print $$1}')
ITERATION := $(shell git describe --tags --long | awk -F "-" '{print $$2}')
TESTFOLDERS := $(shell go list ./... | grep -v /misc/)

all: test

test:
	go test -count=1 -v $(TESTFOLDERS) -cover -race

test-integration:
	go test -count=1 -v $(TESTFOLDERS) -cover -race --tags=integration

generate:
	bash misc/scripts/generate.sh
	go generate ./...
	go run internal/cli/configdoc/main.go

web:
	./misc/scripts/update_web.sh

swagger-web:
	make generate
	./misc/scripts/update_swagger_web.sh

package:
	./misc/scripts/package.sh $(VERSION) $(ITERATION)

sync-websocket:
	rsync -av --exclude '*/' ../centrifuge/internal/websocket/ internal/websocket/

packagecloud:
	make packagecloud-deb
	make packagecloud-rpm

packagecloud-deb:
	# PACKAGECLOUD_TOKEN env must be set
	package_cloud push FZambia/centrifugo/debian/buster PACKAGES/*.deb
	package_cloud push FZambia/centrifugo/debian/bullseye PACKAGES/*.deb
	package_cloud push FZambia/centrifugo/debian/bookworm PACKAGES/*.deb

	package_cloud push FZambia/centrifugo/ubuntu/focal PACKAGES/*.deb
	package_cloud push FZambia/centrifugo/ubuntu/jammy PACKAGES/*.deb
	package_cloud push FZambia/centrifugo/ubuntu/noble PACKAGES/*.deb

packagecloud-rpm:
	# PACKAGECLOUD_TOKEN env must be set
	package_cloud push FZambia/centrifugo/el/7 PACKAGES/*.rpm

update-deps:
	./misc/scripts/update-deps.sh

deps:
	go mod tidy

local-deps:
	go mod tidy
	go mod download
	go mod vendor

GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION := $(shell cat .golangci-lint-version)

lint:
	@if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		echo "golangci-lint not found. Run 'make install-lint' to install $(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION)."; \
		exit 1; \
	fi
	@INSTALLED=$$(golangci-lint version --short 2>/dev/null); \
	EXPECTED=$$(echo $(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION) | sed 's/^v//'); \
	if [ "$${INSTALLED%%.*}" != "$${EXPECTED%%.*}" ] || [ "$${INSTALLED#*.}" != "$${EXPECTED#*.}" ]; then \
		echo "golangci-lint version mismatch: installed $${INSTALLED}, expected $${EXPECTED}. Run 'make install-lint'."; \
		exit 1; \
	fi
	golangci-lint run --timeout 10m0s --verbose

install-lint:
	go install github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/v2/cmd/golangci-lint@$(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION)

vuln:
	@if ! command -v govulncheck >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		echo "govulncheck not found, installing..."; \
		go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest; \
	fi
	govulncheck ./...

build:
	CGO_ENABLED=0 go build

content.text:
The feature implementation is complete. Here's a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

### Changes in `internal/configtypes/types.go`
Added two new configuration fields to `KafkaConsumerConfig`:
- **`CredentialsProfile`** - A shared AWS credentials profile to use for MSK IAM authentication (useful when running on EC2 or with a local credentials file)
- **`AssumeRoleARN`** - When provided, causes the consumer to assume the specified IAM role using AWS STS, which is needed for cross-account MSK access

These fields mirror the existing configuration in the SQS consumer (`AwsSqsConsumerConfig`), providing parity between the two consumers.

### Changes in `internal/consuming/kafka.go`
- Added imports for `aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/aws` (aliased as `awssdk`), `awsconfig`, `stscreds`, and `sts` - the same AWS SDK packages used by the SQS consumer
- Updated the `aws-msk-iam` SASL mechanism case in `initClient()`:
  - **When `AssumeRoleARN` is set**: Loads the AWS default config (with optional profile via `CredentialsProfile`), creates an STS client, uses `stscreds.NewAssumeRoleProvider` to assume the specified role, wraps credentials in `awssdk.NewCredentialsCache` for automatic renewal, and uses `aws.ManagedStreamingIAM()` with a callback to dynamically provide temporary credentials (including `SessionToken`) on each SASL authentication
  - **When `AssumeRoleARN` is not set** (backward compatible): Uses the original approach with direct `AccessKey`/`SecretKey` from `SASLUser`/`SASLPassword` via `AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism()`

### Key design decisions:
- **Backward compatibility**: If `AssumeRoleARN` is not set, the original behavior is preserved exactly
- **Credential rotation**: Using `ManagedStreamingIAM` (callback-based) instead of `AsManagedStreamingIAMMechanism` (static) when STS is used ensures that if SASL re-authentication is needed, fresh temporary credentials are fetched rather than stale ones
- **Consistency with SQS consumer**: The same AWS SDK pattern (`stscreds.NewAssumeRoleProvider` + `aws.NewCredentialsCache`) is used
